videos.desc: I met the inventor of Kid Pix and we made a video. #kidpix It’s linked on my YT. 🎶🖋
videos.text: Hi everybody it's Jeffrey here and I wanted to tell you about a video that I made about a half year ago about the creator of the computer program, a kid's painting program called Kidpix and I made a film with a creator, a video with the creator of Kidpix where I interviewed him and we talked about art and drawing and creativity and things like that. So you can go to my YouTube channel and you can actually watch the video now and it's really really good if you ever used Kidpix when you were little. It's something that inspired me and I really loved it and Craig the Inventor is one of my heroes and I just love Kidpix so much that all my drawings and things that I make on this channel they include sound and stuff and a big part of that reason is because of Kidpix. Okay well I hope you enjoy the video. Talk to you soon. Goodbye.

videos.desc: drawing is the best videogame 📗🖋
videos.text: drawing is kind of like the best video game and like I mean if the sketchbook is like your screen or like your game console and these are all like your levels you know then the pen is basically like your controller and you can do like all kinds of stuff you can play games with people you can have fun or you can get really frustrated but basically anything you can do in a video game you can do in a little drawing and you can do it anywhere without batteries and it's also existed for like I don't know thousands of years and people used to do it on the walls of caves and stuff like that and so when you draw you're kind of also communicating with the caveman in that sense you're playing the same game that they played back in the day

videos.text: I just woke up from a bad dream and it was a dream where I was friends with people and then I wasn't friends with them anymore because I was a little bit too different from them and they were like popular but I wasn't as popular just different and like so then I kind of got cast out from there from their friends and and like yeah I mean sometimes you like have to say goodbye to people and stuff where you have bad dreams here's a little fish that like can go both ways because they have two tails and they're in a little nightmare themselves because they only have one tail in real life but in the dream they have two tails and they don't know which way to go

videos.desc: an introduction to digital painting theory for all my art friends 👾
videos.text: So here's a bit of digital painting theory for you guys. Now, if you were to create a, the simplest possible digital painting, it would have to only be a one by one pixel image. And that pixel image could either be blank, white, you know, or it could be black. So it could either be not filled in or filled in basically, you know. So it would, so that would be the one by one pixel image. Now, if you up the resolution to two by two, right, you could paint in some different areas of the picture, but it would still be limited, right? There's only so many combinations. And then if you pop open Photoshop, you can have as many pixels as you want. But once you decide on a resolution, that still means there's only going to be a limited number of possibilities. And so the question is, when you're making a digital painting, are you actually making something new or are you simply searching for possibilities?

So basically that's abstraction, and that's just wrong.

videos.desc: @eian_b this should help you understand the use value of abstraction! Without ♾ there’d be no computers, and w/o 🙂, no cartoons, or alphabet❗️
videos.text: Question. Abstract art is just an excuse for non-talented people to do art. Well, let's talk about abstraction in mathematics and see if abstraction is an excuse for non-talented people to do mathematics. So if you have one thing, right, you can have one thing and then two things, right? And we use symbols for those things, like one and two, right? And then you can have three things, right? Now that's very concrete, right? That's concrete mathematics. I can have one, two or three sticks. But what if I want to have infinity sticks, right? Infinity amount of sticks. Nobody has ever seen infinity amount of sticks. So we have a special symbol, an abstraction, in order to do things with something that nobody has ever seen. Now there's some things in art that people have also never seen. Those things are things like circles, triangles, and even a smiley face.

videos.desc: 📜 Drawing on the hidden meanings of lines.✒️🌀 #doodle #aesthetic #art #neverfitin
videos.text: I would like to tell you about the hidden meanings of lines, also known as people. The first of four is the straight line. Many people live their lives in a very straight line. The second, of course, is the curvy line. Many people make lots of twists and turns. The third is a line that already exists, but another person comes along and traces that line, and they live a very similar life as the original line. And the fourth is the dotted line. These people barely live at all. They just sit at home, read books, and play video games, and they project themselves forward into the virtual spaces.

videos.desc: Reply to @acspy72 here is the difference between a drawing, doodle, and sketch! ✒️➿
videos.text: Now, the first thing you need to understand is what a drawing actually is. And a drawing, let's use this mountain do as an example, a drawing is not just a little mark like that, but a drawing is actually a set of marks that have some kind of ordering principle to them, a set of marks that have a pattern. And that's a drawing, but any old animal can make a drawing. For example, if you have a little kitty and it's making a trail and you're trying to follow the trail, well then that cat is making a drawing with its paw prints, but it's not necessarily a doodle or a sketch. Now, a doodle is a type of drawing that you make, the type of pattern of marks that you make when you don't have any ideas and are looking for an idea. Oh, I found a cool idea right here. And a sketch is something that you might do to develop that idea a little bit further.

Anyway what I wanted to talk to you today is about Grand Theft Auto and Billy Eilish and the thing is if you want to be an artist you might see something like Grand Theft Auto and you might want to make something like Grand Theft Auto that's a form of iconographic thinking in which you want to be an artist to match something like Grand Theft Auto. You want to make something as great as that but maybe you get into art because you're interested in someone like Billy Eilish you want to live like them you want to be like them that's a form of idolatry you might get interested in art because you want to be like someone else you idolize them. Now 99% of people are going to be interested in art for these reasons but if you really want to get really good at art you don't want to just do those things that's kind of like vanilla and chocolate you actually want to get into media you want to get into the tools that you use to make art and keep your imagination there.

videos.desc: Reply to @spunksandwich those 5 year olds need to learn how to make cool drawings from somewhere don’t they?
videos.text: Hi, Spunk Sandwich. My name's Depri, and yeah, maybe you don't think I'm a very good artist, but you know, I am an art teacher and an art professor, basically, and you know, I think that it's kind of, you know, the five-year-olds, they got to look somewhere, they got to look up to something, and not everybody's at the same skill level. And also, most of my drawings are actually about making things as simple as possible, as opposed to making things more difficult.

videos.desc: Reply to @butterflycliplady whistlegraphs are a little art form I came up with to address a number of concerns I had with painting and media 🙂
videos.text: a whistle graph is like a form of painting or almost like a poem or a haiku of painting and here's so here's one here that I like I'm a butterfly flapping for you guys it's just a costume I put on in my room and so I'm a I kind of do a lot of work with media and thinking about how can a painting exist for thousands and thousands of years and obviously the painting the object of the painting will get destroyed or most artworks will be destroyed and even digital artworks could eventually be destroyed or deteriorated so I wanted to create something that was a form of painting that somebody could easily memorize and so that it could also be memorized in a distributed way that didn't require the internet so in people's minds and memories and the meaning is always there and it can last for as long as humanity can last.

segments.text: a whistle graph is like a form of painting or almost like a poem or a haiku of painting and here's

eventually be destroyed or deteriorated so I wanted to create something that was a form of
painting that somebody could easily memorize and so that it could also be memorized in a distributed
way that didn't require the internet so in people's minds and memories and the meaning is always there
and it can last for as long as humanity can last.

videos.desc: basic technique: how to draw using invisible lines ✒️🕳 #doodle #drawing #art 🌳
videos.text: not all lines in a drawing are visible. As a matter of fact, some of them are often invisible. So here's an example of a line that's visible, a guiding line that's visible, and also this little wonky line that follows along that guiding line. Now, if you wanna draw a bush, for example, you can start to do a motion with your hand like this, and then follow an invisible guiding line like that, where your line circumscribes the invisible line.

videos.desc: ✒️🕳 scary emotions
videos.text: Sometimes people have a lot of really scary emotions inside and those emotions can express themselves in different ways. A lot of people choose to draw or to smush clay or something like that and shape it into different things when they have those emotions. But some people never learned how to do that and so they start to smush things or push things or press things that might have emotions themselves and that's not really good because then what happens is other people will get upset and then they'll have a lot of strong emotions and they won't know how to express those emotions either and so they'll start to express them in different places. Places that art shouldn't usually go and that can be a really big problem but it's also okay that that happens.

That's all I'm trying to say

videos.text: How can you make art about feelings that you haven't sorted out yet? Well, one interesting thing about art is, you know, all art is basically about feeling and generally the best artwork is about feelings that are not so easily understood, you know. And when you're a little kid, you have a lot of energy and you can make a lot of really energetic artwork, but usually the feelings in that artwork are pretty simple and pretty reproducible. And that tends to be what we call the concept of cliché, which basically means a stamp, something that you can repeat like a meme. But when you get older, you're capable of having more complicated feelings and the best thing to do when you're having complicated feelings that you can't really put your finger on is to actually mediate them and make art about them and communicate them to others and that's basically how you make great work.

segments.text: How can you make art about feelings that you haven't sorted out yet?
Well, one interesting thing about art is, you know, all art is basically about feeling and
generally the best artwork is about feelings that are not so easily understood, you know.
And when you're a little kid, you have a lot of energy and you can make a lot of really
energetic artwork, but usually the feelings in that artwork are pretty simple and pretty reproducible.
And that tends to be what we call the concept of cliché, which basically means a stamp,
something that you can repeat like a meme. But when you get older, you're capable of having more
complicated feelings and the best thing to do when you're having complicated feelings that you can't
really put your finger on is to actually mediate them and make art about them and communicate them
to others and that's basically how you make great work.

videos.text: A lot of people have been telling me that they don't really understand how to use my game No Paint. And No Paint is an imagination training game. When you open it up you tap here and it will start blank but this is a painting I'm already working on. And what you can do is it starts to paint something. If you don't like what it's painting you can say no but you can also slide your finger over to compare the difference. And you have to play it with an open mind. You have to think about do I really like this thing that's being made. And if you believe in it strong enough then it does work and you can actually express yourself within No Paint.

videos.desc: How to use commands in No Paint. 📓 Did you already know this? ✒️🎶
videos.text: Not many people know this, but no paint actually has commands built in. So any brush that you see, like build, for example, if you tap there, then you can actually loop that brush by tapping here. And then it will continue to only do build brushes, and you can make a whole painting using build. You can also reset your painting by just tapping reset and then during the reset command.

videos.desc: The difference between analog & digital stuff. ➿☑️ #edutok #art #mediation #media #technology
videos.text: Do you want to know the difference between analog stuff and digital stuff? Well, guess what? Because I'm your guy. So analog stuff basically goes like this. I notice how I'm just doing this one thing. You don't really know, like, if I ask you how many, how many am I doing, you can't answer, right? But if I start to do this, then all of a sudden you can say one, two, three, four. And that's basically the difference between something that's analog and something that's digital. Analog things are all about unity, about oneness. digital things can be divided. It doesn't really have anything to do with computers or electronics.

videos.desc: good afternoon 🙂 all my pals out there - here is another little claim on digital painting #theory ✒️🎶
videos.text: You will probably find this rather odd, but you don't actually need a computer in order to make a digital painting. All you really have to do is decide what constitutes information and what constitutes ink. So right here, the ink doesn't really matter because I'm primarily concerned with the information that I'm putting down, which is a grid of four squares that can either be filled in or not filled in, and that is what makes a painting digital. It doesn't really have anything to do with computers.

which is a grid of four squares that can either be filled in or not filled in,
and that is what makes a painting digital. It doesn't really have anything to do with computers.

videos.desc: drawing is the best videogame ✒️🙂
videos.text: Alright, check this out. This is a Nintendo DS. And here is a little piece of bark. And when I was little, I used to shove this thing in the back thinking it would create some kind of video game, like I could play the inside of the bark. And would not be an amazing game console idea? I mean, I think it would be. But actually, as I got older, I realized that all I really needed to do that was a pen. Because if I just look at some cool objects, like my cat for example, and then I try to draw in my sketchbook, then it's basically the same thing. And drawing is kind of like the best video game ever, as long as there's not a cat here flying around everywhere.

videos.desc: Reply to @daniel_prew attention is what revs this whole engine ⏱ #edutok #media #art #tiktok #WelcomeWeek
videos.text: I would like to tell you guys about the meaning of TikTok. Now, think about the sound, TikTok. Of course, it reminds you of a clock. And what is TikTok? TikTok is social media. And what do you do on social media? You check it throughout the day. And so the clock was really the very first type of social media. And what really is social media? Social media is a way of organizing people. In a way of keeping people together and keeping their thoughts and actions and behaviors together. And so when you hear things about, oh, social media is controlling us or controlling your brain, yes, that's actually extremely true. But it all began with that bid for attention. The clock always bags for your attention.

videos.desc: my thoughts on the symbolism of @charlidamelio's drink and what it means for kids to order one
videos.text: So I've been thinking about Charlie's Dunkin Donuts drink and it reminds me of this story about like when I was little I used to watch this cereal commercial for cocoa puffs and the cocoa puffs commercial had a cartoon and in the cartoon when they ate the cocoa puffs they would blast off into outer space and I was at my cousin's house and they didn't have cocoa puffs they had cocoa crispies I was never allowed cocoa puffs so I had a bite of the cocoa crispies and then I like jumped up and was like and I was like wow I'm finally living the whole commercial like I am the cocoa puffs the result of cocoa puffs and I was wondering if people who buy Charlie's drink like kids who buy Charlie's drink they want to dance like Charlie and so the drink symbolically helps them to dance better.

videos.desc: how much information is necessary for you to express yourself?
videos.text: Have you ever thought about how much information is needed in order for you to express yourself? Like for example, if you just were making a drawing and it could only be blank or filled in, like if it could only be like a zero or a one, like is that much information? Is that enough information for you to express yourself or do you need more? Like can you actually express yourself if you divide it into four? Or what if you divide it into 16? Can you express yourself then? I can start to, like for example, I can start to express an emotion. Once I have 16, I can actually make something happy like that.

videos.desc: draw urself a line and use it to think about stuff
videos.text: The most interesting drawing that you can make right now is a simple drawing of a line. Because once you draw a line, you might wonder why there's two sides to the line. And you might start to think about what that line might be like. Like is it thick or thin? Is the space around the line really big? Or is it really small? And once you can kind of understand what that line sort of means for yourself, you're also closer to understanding, well, yourself at that point in time. And the kinds of things that are going on in your mind, and the kinds of lines that you're drawing, or not drawing, inside.

Or is it really small?
And once you can kind of understand what that line sort of means for yourself,
you're also closer to understanding, well, yourself at that point in time.
And the kinds of things that are going on in your mind,
and the kinds of lines that you're drawing, or not drawing, inside.

videos.desc: on the seriousness of fingerpainting
videos.text: When I was a kid they kind of pushed you know the grown-ups kind of pushed finger painting on me and they were like this is very serious Jeffrey this is something that you should really be spending a lot of your time on and I believed them I was like yeah this is this is very interesting and I had a lot of fun doing it and I really spent a lot of time on it but then one day they took it away and they were like this is no longer serious this is no longer something that's worth your time and I believe them for a long time but recently I rediscovered finger painting and I realized how interesting it actually is and how grown-up it is and how complicated it is and if you look at anyone today who has a smartphone and you watch their finger move you can see that that that it is a very serious thing and the fact that you're moving your finger all the time but you're not keeping track of the movements just shows how people have kind of forgotten the meaning of painting with your finger

videos.desc: on dots and lines and perception ✒️🕳
videos.text: The difference between dots and lines is only perceptual. For example, if I were to make some dots here, notice how I was actually creating a movement with my pen, kind of like that and see how that matches up. So even dots themselves have hidden invisible lines, not unlike how if you just bounce around inside a shape, the dots would be the areas in which you made contact with the outside.

videos.desc: this lecture was for the birds in ny backyard
videos.text: When you type something into the Google search engine you get results for the information that you're looking for. And Google's slogan used to be "Don't be evil" because they knew how important it is so that if someone's searching for information you'll give them the right information. Now what I think is pretty interesting is that often people come to you because they're seeking information and I think a good slogan for people would be "Don't be evil" when people are trying to seek information from you. You should really understand what are they searching for and what can you actually give them.

videos.desc: Reply to @jackaboyyolo26 this is what's special about my pen and just dedicating myself to a single pen in general ✒️🙂 #drawing #pen #sketchbook #art
videos.text: I used to carry a lot of pens, and not worry too much about what pen I was using, but ever since I started just using one pen, I've realized that there's some cool things about using one pen. For example, if my pen is somewhere else, then I start to have more of an awareness of where that pen was, and if I have it on me, I feel a little bit more powerful than if I don't have it on me. And also, because I'm forced to just use this one pen all the time, I can come up with different things to do with the pen, you know, like a whistle graph. Would you like to come down the stairs, I don't want to, because I'm too scared. And I would almost say that the reason why I invented whistle graphs is because I chose to only stick to one pen. And then now that I'm sticking to one pen, I'm like, well, what is it that I can actually do with this thing? And so I think that just choosing to have one thing gives you both an awareness of that thing and makes you more creative.

videos.desc: Reply to @official_ameliia here is a quick explanation of what i was thinking about when coming up with a name 🙃 #art #doodle #drawing
videos.text: The reason I call whistle graphs whistle graphs is because not because I whistle or don't whistle in them, but because whistling is primarily a leisurely activity, and so it's about fun and play, and graph is kind of more of a serious activity that's more about mathematics and sort of putting something down on paper that's in your mind, putting down some information. But one thing that's interesting about whistling is whistling also creates a perfect sine wave, which is something that synthesizer types of people are interested in, and I think whistle graphs are kind of a futuristic form of computer art in which you no longer need a computer to use them. But you know what, I also do whistle in some of them, like this one.

videos.desc: only draw this line if you want your art to be dead on arrival 🙂 #artprofessor #drawing #theory #doodle
videos.text: One of the most dangerous lines that you can draw is the line between your artwork and your life because once you make that distinction, like let's say maybe you have separate Instagram accounts, one for your art and one for your life, once you make that distinction, you no longer can include your life in your artwork and so by definition you're kind of excluding the life and squeezing it out of your art.

videos.desc: don't limit your idea of generative art to "computer software that generates stuff" because there is a whole lot more to it than that! ✒️ #doodle #art
videos.text: Generative art is kind of an interesting topic because a lot of people I know would think of generative art as requiring a computer of some kind to draw little dots or to procedurally generate different kinds of things. However, generative art doesn't necessarily have to be using a computer. And actually you can do a lot of things with generative art that is very very difficult to do using a computer or by making software. For example, if you were to draw this square shape here, and then you wanted to transform the shape, you can do that very simply. You can just squash the shape. But what if you wanted to stretch the shape, right? You can stretch the shape like this. And these are basically the the ideas behind generative art. You don't really need to use a computer to draw cool lines and have them dancing around. You can actually just use the computer in your brain, so to speak, in order to develop new interesting forms in computations.

square shape here, and then you wanted to transform the shape,
you can do that very simply. You can just squash the shape.
But what if you wanted to stretch the shape, right? You can stretch the shape like this.
And these are basically the the ideas behind generative art.
You don't really need to use a computer to draw cool lines and have them dancing around.
You can actually just use the computer in your brain, so to speak, in order to develop new interesting forms in
computations.

videos.desc: here is my art review of starry night by vincent van gogh #artschool #painting #vangogh
videos.text: Here's my review of the Starry Night by Vincent van Gogh, 1889. This painting existed at a time in which culture was still trying to simulate reality in paint using images, but van Gogh was interested in creating action and not just creating an image of action, but using action itself as part of the picture making process.

This painting existed at a time in which culture was still trying to simulate reality
in paint using images, but van Gogh was interested in creating action and not just creating an

I think it's cute piano music and it's somber.

videos.desc: Reply to @uh.sophiab_ here is part 1 of why it's called whistlegraphing ✒️🎶 do u need part 2? #art #graph #invention #doodle #artschool 😴✨
videos.text: So how did I come up with the name whistlegraph? Well you see there's such a thing as a bar graph and a bar graph allows you to compare the quantities of different things. There's also such a thing as a telegraph and a telegraph allows you to send a message from one place to another really far in space time. But of course a whistlegraph lately when I fly my heart lies low as I get high after we touch down I pick it up from off the ground is kind of like a painting that can be memorized over time and space and it has a lot of feeling involved and it's synchronized too.

segments.text: So how did I come up with the name whistlegraph? Well you see there's such a thing as a bar graph
and a bar graph allows you to compare the quantities of different things. There's also
such a thing as a telegraph and a telegraph allows you to send a message from one place
to another really far in space time. But of course a whistlegraph lately when I fly
my heart lies low as I get high after we touch down I pick it up from off the ground
is kind of like a painting that can be memorized over time and space and it has a lot of feeling
involved and it's synchronized too.

videos.desc: Reply to @herrokitty2000 this is what i think of geccing ✒️☺️ #geccing #artschool #drawing #edutok #100gecs
videos.text: Gekking comes from the band 100geks, and when 100geks found some graffiti on a wall that said 100geks, they're like, "What the heck does that mean?" And a lot of their music goes, "Gek, Gek, Gek, Gek, Gek, Gek, Gek, Gek, Gek, Gek." And so the graffiti they found on the wall was a little bit like an accident, an accidental find. And the Gekking, "Gek, Gek, Gek, Gek, Gek, Gek," is a little bit like a glitch, an accidental sound. It happens when you turn an accident back around and say, "Oh, I really appreciate accidents, and I want to create a whole art form out of the aesthetics of accidents." That's basically what Gekking is. And accidents occur in other kinds of art, too. For example, in painting, if you leave a drip and the drip drops down on the canvas, that is also a kind of Gekking.

videos.desc: Reply to @the_magestic_alicorn_ i'm a yale person, not a harvard person! #education #artschool #graphicscore evolution! #art #composition ✒️☺️
videos.text: No, I didn't go to Harvard, but I actually did go to Yale for a sculpture as a matter of fact, for my master's degree. So I have been to art school. And as a matter of fact, whistle graphs are part of my sort of contemporary art research. And the technical term for a whistle graph is actually something called a graphic score. So in contemporary music composition, there's the concept of the graphic score, which is a kind of picture that you look at in order to play music from. And a whistle graph is more of a graphic instrument, in which it's a picture that you look at, but it also is the music itself.

videos.desc: How to draw more confidently and more comfortably! #edutok #drawing #art #artschool #lineart ✒️☺️
videos.text: A lot of people, when they first start to draw, they think in terms of the tip of the pen, and they imagine, and keep their imagination right where the tip of the pen is. And so when they draw, you'll see them going back and forth, like this, trying desperately to shape a line. But if you think about the pen more like driving a car, and you just drive along at the same speed, making your turns, trying to arrive at some future destination, then not only will you seem more confident, but you'll also be a lot more comfy.

segments.text: A lot of people, when they first start to draw, they think in terms of the tip of the pen,
and they imagine, and keep their imagination right where the tip of the pen is.
And so when they draw, you'll see them going back and forth, like this, trying desperately
to shape a line.
But if you think about the pen more like driving a car, and you just drive along at the same
speed, making your turns, trying to arrive at some future destination, then not only
will you seem more confident, but you'll also be a lot more comfy.

But now I am the ghost of your high beam, easily confused for a cloud of exhaust.

And you would say, oh Jeffrey, that's not a good whistle graph. That's a bad whistle graph. And you would be right. That's incorrect. Whereas this is correct. But what about abstraction? What about abstract painting? That would be something like this. Would you like to go down the stairs? I don't want to cause I'm too scared. Woo! So notice how I spatially, like the marks are spatially weird, but I did get some things correct. And that's why people often argue about abstract painting. So basically that's abstraction and that's just wrong.

videos.desc: Reply to @harrisonwyrick maybe this helps u understand what studying sculpture meant for me. Also my undergrad was at Ringling. #artschool #sculpture
videos.text: A sculpture is basically a way of appreciating space, right, and there's all different kinds of spaces in the world. There's one-dimensional space, two-dimensional space, three-dimensional space, and so to study sculpture is to study the appreciation and the like qualities of space. And so when I think about sculpture, I think of painting, for example, as a genre of sculpture. I think of whistle graphs as a kind of sculpture. I even think of like making TikToks in like a sculptural way because it's all about appreciating space and time in new ways, and that's really what sculpture at kind of a more conceptual level is all about.

videos.desc: Reply to @sailorlavender here is a little behind tbe scenes of what developing a whistlegraph is like for me! #doodle #art #drawing #development ✒️🎶
videos.text: whistle graphs actually take quite a lot of work, and so you can see all these sheets that you're looking at here, every single one of these pages are all about the development of one single whistle graph, all these pages here. And then when I develop it and memorize it, then I have to actually record it, and then there's multiple takes that have to get recorded in order to get a good video. And so that can be really challenging. And I do this for almost every video, there's multiple drawings that get made in order to actually do the thing. But then, once it's all done, all you have to do is something like this. Just visualizing flowers in my mind. Maybe one day you will do it with me here.

videos.text: I really had a great childhood, but even though I had a lot of room to play and run around, I didn't really have a whole lot of options in my life. For example, I didn't get to choose my classes at school, if my parents made me food, I just had to eat it, and they would buy stuff for my room, and I didn't really have much choice there. And so I ended up playing a lot of video games, and what video games allowed me to do is really exercise choice and have agency over my life. And as I grew up, I became less and less interested in them, because I started to be able to do things like choose where I went to college, choose my classes, choose what I wanted to do and who I wanted to hang out with, and the more choices I had in my life, the less I got interested in playing them.

videos.text: Here's the character Sid from Toy Story, and this is Andy, who's the good guy, but Andy believes in full ownership of his toys. He brands them with his name, kind of like plantation owners would brand their slaves, whereas Sid dismantles toys to make new ones. He's actually more of an artist or an inventor, and creative energy is supposed to be kind of chaotic at times. If anything, I choose Sid.

videos.text: Even though I really appreciate my childhood, I didn't have a whole lot of options in my life. I didn't get to choose where to go to school or what I had to eat, stuff like that. And I played a lot of video games because video games allowed me to actually make real choices and have real consequences. As I grew up, though, I didn't really need to play them that much anymore and now I only really play them for short periods of time because, well, I can do things in my life.

videos.desc: Reply to @flame_ash11 it will be a great job in about 20 years! 📅 #drawing #whistlegraphing #career #job #artist
videos.text: Are you asking this because you think whistlegrafting is really cool and you want it to be your job? Well, let me tell you something. Yes, actually, whistlegrafting will be a job, but it's not a job right now. Because in the 80s, there was all these artists, and these artists had really weird ideas. And what they tried to do is all this weird stuff with video cameras, and a lot of that involved like filming themselves and doing like, you know, crazy stuff. But basically, that's the stuff we do on TikTok today. So I kind of think whistlegrafting is kind of a crazy idea, but maybe in the future, machines will have to like, talk to us, and the only way we'll be able to talk to each other is like, through whistling and through doing whistlegraphs, so that they'll like, because it might be one of the only things that like, supercomputers of the future like, can't figure out.

But basically, that's the stuff we do on TikTok today.
So I kind of think whistlegrafting is kind of a crazy idea, but maybe in the future,
machines will have to like, talk to us, and the only way we'll be able to talk to each other
is like, through whistling and through doing whistlegraphs, so that they'll like,
because it might be one of the only things that like, supercomputers of the future like, can't figure out.

videos.text: One of the coolest things about whistle graphs is that they're memorizable. So one day, if someone says, "Hey, you're an artist, let me see your work. "I don't have to pull out my phone. "All I have to do is something like this." ♪ Well, well, well ♪ ♪ Look who it is ♪ ♪ Crawling in the corner of the room ♪ And then I'd be like, that's one of my pieces. What do you think about that picture? And I don't have to show them an image of a painting or anything.

videos.desc: Here's how art works across disciplines and why artists play an important role in culture. #art #edutok #artschool #diagram #notebook ✒️♾
videos.text: So what do artists actually do? These are pyramids of fields like piano, gymnastics, math and in any of these pyramids you're often presented with a novice and a pro option and you can climb that pyramid to try to become a pro but what artists often do is they begin to climb a pyramid and then they change their mind and then they might build a little bridge across from one pyramid to another and if you look at a society the best artists are often found right here on the bridges and eventually people come up and they build a new pyramid.

segments.text: So what do artists actually do? These are pyramids of fields like piano, gymnastics,
math and in any of these pyramids you're often presented with a novice and a pro option
and you can climb that pyramid to try to become a pro but what artists often do is they begin to
climb a pyramid and then they change their mind and then they might build a little bridge across
from one pyramid to another and if you look at a society the best artists are often found
right here on the bridges and eventually people come up and they build a new pyramid.

videos.desc: Thoughts on fingerpainting. 👉🎨 #art #fingerpainting #aesthetic #painting #contemporaryart
videos.text: Back when I was a kid, I used to love the shit out of finger painting you guys, but as I grew up, the grown-ups kind of told me I should use paint brushes and more serious shit. However, now that I'm fully aware, I've realized how cool finger painting is as a grown-up. And if you think about it, every time you use your phone, you're kind of finger painting too. You're just not leaving a trace.

videos.text: I'm sorry, you feel this way a big Korean bowtoe, but I want to talk for a second about what the term "scam" means. Because a scam is basically something that, well, you think it's one thing, for example, like a circle. But actually, it's a square, and you've been tricked. And what most art is, is actually most art is a scam, because you think that something might be a smiley face, but in fact, it's ink on a page.

videos.desc: College level media theory about TikTok, Google, and the future of the Internet. #tiktok #google #internet #mediatheory #lineart ✒️💭
videos.text: Hey guys, it's WhistlerGraph. I want to talk about some college-level media theory. So if Google was the interface of the Internet of the 90s and the 10s, you know, the text box, it assumed that you knew what to search for. But most people actually don't really know what they want. And so, TikTokers are basically human information clerks. We mostly tell you what to Google. And it's the new front door of Gen Z's Internet, soon to be everyone else's.

videos.desc: Reply to @neptuniamonnaanarchus it's not really my place 2 classify them but it is my job to develop em 🙂 #arthistory #lineart #artist #conceptualart
videos.text: So conceptual art was a movement in the 70s that was responding to changes in technology, especially computers in which they were trying to figure out whether artwork could be thought of as pure information. And all whistle graphs have these instructions and they are really pieces of information because if you can perform the instructions then you've basically created the whistle graph. So whistle graphs are kind of like performable paintings in a sense and they owe a lot of their history to conceptual art. Can you actually do conceptual art today is a more interesting question because it did happen in the 70s and whatever comes after that. And there's many things that have come after that you can't really kind of say you're doing conceptual art because well so many things have happened and that just that term or that phrase conceptual art really refers to a specific period in time.

segments.text: So conceptual art was a movement in the 70s that was responding to changes in technology, especially computers in which they were trying to figure out whether artwork could be thought of as pure information.
And all whistle graphs have these instructions and they are really pieces of information because if you can perform the instructions then you've basically created the whistle graph.
So whistle graphs are kind of like performable paintings in a sense and they owe a lot of their history to conceptual art.
Can you actually do conceptual art today is a more interesting question because it did happen in the 70s and whatever comes after that.

And there's many things that have come after that you can't really kind of say you're doing conceptual art because well so many things have happened and that just that term or that phrase conceptual art really refers to a specific period in time.

videos.text: "They would have drawn furry arts to make ends meet." I don't think anybody would have hired Vincent Vengo to be a furry artist because honestly, the man was a terrible draftsman. He did come up with this really unique and idiosyncratic way of making marks and that's kind of what he's known for. That's part of the beauty of him as an artist. But I know this stuff because I'm also really bad. I can't draw for shit. And one of the differences between illustrators and artists is that artists kind of, they're kind of incapable. They have to come up with their own means in order to survive and get through life. Whereas illustrators tend to be really good at known ways of doing things. They're really capable and skilled people in that sense.

videos.desc: My little invention for memorizable cinema. ✒️🎶 #mediatheory #arthistory #technology #doodle #cinema
videos.text: This is an old film strip in which they put the sound right along the side of the image. Images are stored on one side and sound on the other. They run in parallel. Twisting them together creates a single braid. Yielding a memorizable and manual form of cinema that I call the Whistlegraph. Would you like to come down the stairs? I don't want to 'cause I'm too scared.

I don't want to 'cause I'm too scared.

videos.desc: behind the scenes of frog tiara #doodle #aesthetic #artist #makingof #whistlegraph
videos.text: Here's how I made the Frog Tiara whistle graph. First I wanted to draw a frog, but I didn't really know how to do a lily pad So I thought it could be a cool frog hat on a kid I thought maybe the kid would be going to sleep and it was time for them to get to bed and be tucked in I Thought about drawing the whole bed, but it seemed to take up too much squary space So I decided to do a little curve instead and add a moon and a star and I was almost finished But I did a bit more work until I finally got Exactly what I wanted I wear my frog tiara Above my head it keeps away the nightmares When I go to bed

segments.text: Here's how I made the Frog Tiara whistle graph. First I wanted to draw a frog, but I didn't really know how to do a lily pad

videos.desc: A new interface for making interpretive binary decisions in role-playing games that aren't so cut & dry! #art #rpg #dungeonsanddragons #doodle #games
videos.text: Earlier tonight I was playing a role-playing game with my friend Alex and I was thinking about how in role-playing games you often have to make a chaotic decision like oh will you make the jump or not make the jump or are they happy or are they sad and you can usually flip a coin in order to do this but the coin flip is so jarring it's so it's just one thing or the other thing so I was thinking there must be a better way to make decisions in role-playing games to make binary decisions and so I figured if you fold a piece of paper in half and then you have your two options on the top and bottom and then you smash then not only will you have an idea of what the result of the decision is but you'll be able to also kind of interpret okay how did that decision get made are we happy right now or are we sad

decision get made are we happy right now or are we sad

videos.desc: Pop stars get bossed around by their culture. Artists boss their culture around. 🎥✍️ #art #culture #aesthetics #contemporaryart #mythology
videos.text: Let's compare the myth of the pop star to the myth of the artist. Pop stars basically exist in a culture and the culture kind of dictates or tells them what to do. In other words, mainstream culture is kind of their boss. Now, when pop stars are no longer able to satisfy the demand that the culture places on them, they get chewed up and spit out. And we've seen this countless times where celebrities will kind of age out and kind of get washed up. Whereas artists, the myth of the artists is that they tend to exist on the outside of society and they are actually so demanding that they want culture to conform to their ideas, to their interests. And this is why art is so kind of like on the edge of madness and why artists seem crazy because they are actually crazy. They're kind of these crazy dictators.

videos.desc: u all know i got my kubrick stare on point
videos.text: How do you quickly make a character look psychotic? It's easy. Just use the Kubrick stare. All it requires is two steps. First, tilt the head slightly downward. Second, stare forward through the eyebrows. It's a technique named after director Stanley Kubrick, who would often explore the concept of dehumanization in his films, stripping away the very essence of what made the character human. It can be used in so many ways. It can be a killing machine in full metal jacket, a deranged psychopath in the shining, a drugged up gang member in a clockwork orange, or it could even be an artificial intelligence gone rogue in 2001's Space Odyssey.

videos.desc: definition of art
videos.text: "Bicachu." Art is basically about as being as weird as possible. Because most people grow up and they try not to be too weird. But if you try to be weird in different ways, then you have to look inside your own mind and think about how to do things differently than other people. And being an artist is kind of like being an expert in being weird.

videos.desc: Reply to @bxnnie...official it's a human readable file format, kind of like haiku #artschool #blockchain #crypto #drawing #lecture
videos.text: All art has to travel through time and space in order to be valuable for the future. And whistle graphs are kind of like a file format for storing animated drawings with poetry over time. So whistle graphs have these different steps, and each step has a different gesture to go with it, and in that you can put some meaning. Well, well, well, look who it is crawling in the corner of the room. And it will last for a really long time because a computer doesn't have to store it, and people's memories can store it in a distributed way. A little bit like a blockchain, but all in the minds of people.

videos.desc: why u should study art & art history and what makes it special 🙂
videos.text: If you're trying to figure out, like, what you should study in college or whatever, then maybe you should study art, because art's about total freedom. It's the ultimate safe space. It actually doesn't matter if you voted for Trump or if you hate sandwiches or love sandwiches or whatever. Basically, you can do whatever you want. And unlike science and technology, which kind of moves in one direction, going up in a curve, art and culture can kind of bounce around in all different places. You know, at some point, there could be a fascist who closes things down and bans homosexuality. At another point, you know, everybody could be having sex on the street and that would be okay. But art's basically about freedom and about moving culture where you want it to go. So if you have ideas about how you think the culture should be, then being an artist is kind of one of the best ways of, well, making those ideas actually reality.

videos.desc: Answer to @gio_logic we use principles rather than rules because it's better for the evolution of the art form ✒️❤️ #artschool
videos.text: There aren't really rules for whistle graphs, but there are principles, and there's a difference. Now watch this one. I miss my girlfriend, she loves to kiss me, and always sends me the best emo G. So see how every single line I drew made a sound, and also when I turn the line in different ways, it tends to make different sounds. Would you like to come down the stairs? I don't want- you get the picture, right? So that's one of the best sort of guidelines for a whistle graph, but sometimes we break the rules too, like in this one. When I'm sad I whip my pen, then I think of something like a little panda bear. Something like a little panda bear.

videos.desc: a nice way of thinking graphically about this existential question 🙂🤌
videos.text: does life actually have a point? Now when it comes to drawing you can make a point on your page like this. Now I want you to stare at this point and think about it for a second. This point could represent something like a hole in a surface so you have kind of a material and then you would fall down the hole or you could have an empty space and this point could represent like a rock. You know maybe something you have to climb over and so this creates a binary system in which life either has a point or doesn't and if you think in this way you're always going to be trapped if you turn life into kind of a light switch.

videos.desc: how to change ur ink! #fountainpen #ink #art
videos.text: It's time to change the ink in my fountain pen. Taking off the cap, and taking the cap off my pen. Unscrewing the nib and the cartridge. Pushing down the piston, dipping it in the ink all the way. And sucking that ink up by screwing the piston back in. Now we have to wipe off the whole excess from the nib. Put the back back on, put the cap back on, give it a little whip. And we're ready to go.

segments.text: It's time to change the ink in my fountain pen.
Taking off the cap, and taking the cap off my pen.
Unscrewing the nib and the cartridge.
Pushing down the piston, dipping it in the ink all the way.
And sucking that ink up by screwing the piston back in.
Now we have to wipe off the whole excess from the nib.
Put the back back on, put the cap back on, give it a little whip.
And we're ready to go.

videos.desc: how to make a digital painting with jelly beans on a post-it
videos.text: If you can't afford Procreate and an iPad Pro, you can still make a digital painting. All you have to do is find discrete units that you can use as information in a nice frame. Then you can place the units around in a certain kind of a visual configuration. And once you have a configuration set in stone, you can always communicate that across a digital network like I'm doing right now.

videos.desc: Answer to @psychogamer_1337 ty for the kind words and keep on drawing in ur own way! ✒️🎶♥️ #artist #inspiration #motivation #lecture
videos.text: You know we work really hard on whistle graphs. It takes us a while to develop each one. This is one that's about a girl who eats flowers. It's not finished yet but should be pretty cool once it's ready. And yeah one thing that I want to say about about drawing pictures is that drawing is kind of one of the best ways that you can have control over your visual environment. So sometimes you might be a kid and you know your parents might be telling you what you have to watch or you know you don't really have control over what you can do on the computer. But if you can find a piece of paper then you can have total visual control and start to develop your own style and your own aesthetic. And that's what I'm really here for. I'm really here to help people find their their own visual path in the world.

videos.desc: what do you think about this digital painting? 🔎 🟨 #painting #digitalart #theory #oilpaint
videos.text: For this digital painting, my picture elements, or pixels, are defined by the tip of this knitting needle. This knitting needle defines my resolution.

segments.text: For this digital painting, my picture elements, or pixels, are defined by the tip of this knitting needle.
This knitting needle defines my resolution.

videos.desc: Reply to @quantumempath I’m sorry everyone, I’m still learning 🍼 🐣
videos.text: The knitting needle can't define both my pixel size and my resolution at the same time, that's just incorrect. If you knew the size of my canvas and the maximum number of knitting needle dots I could place on that canvas, you might have an integer that would stand for the resolution. But otherwise, you don't have the resolution. It's not defined.

videos.desc: Reply to @shsbdydbs3 here's my thoughts on "cringe" #theory #media #cringe #lecture
videos.text: When you see a piece of content and it makes you cringe, it's usually for one of two reasons. The first is that you know the thing so well that it embarrasses you and you feel sympathy for the subject. Classic example being someone trying to act super sexy, but they're really obviously not sexy at all. Now the second one is a little bit harder to pin down. This is one where you might think you know the thing, but actually you've never experienced anything like it. It's totally outside of your worldview, and this is something that's actually assaulting your own value system. And you'll label it as cringe thinking you know the thing when in reality you know nothing about it at all. And technically that's also a definition of conservatism.

videos.desc: Reply to @escargoing.gone here's how I have come to understand writing and how it relates to my work! #writing #poetry #artschool #lecture
videos.text: Here are my thoughts on written language. Even though the letters you write are two-dimensional, the lines you read are one-dimensional. This one-dimensionality is easy to demonstrate in this poem by Gertrude Stein. A rose is a rose is a rose is a rose is a rose. It doesn't matter what direction you enter from, you get stuck in the same linear loop and there's nothing magic about it. Contrast this with pictures which you can enter from any direction and have a nonlinear understanding of. Written language is a technology that wins at sequencing linear thought. But spatial thought is best explored in pictures. In conclusion, writing takes the guesswork or the spatial magic away from pictures. And magic is the key word here. Magic being the root word of image or imagination, which is a spatial thing that we have in our brains.

videos.desc: What's your default drawing mode? … I bet you know mine by now! ✒️🙂
videos.text: I want to tell you about drawing modes. Now, these are ways that you can draw. So the first one is doodling. You can doodle anything you want, as long as you have no idea what you're doing. Next is sketching. Sketching is for getting a rough idea of what you might want to do. Now, writing, that's for sending verbal commands and telling people what to do. Tracing is when you already have something down and you need to follow along. Diagramming is how you make that money. That's how you go from point A to point B and make all the dollars and keep track of things. Whistle graphing is different though. That's for leisure and enjoyment and it goes like this. (whistling)

videos.desc: Answer to @unh0lyboy ✒️🥰 #edutok #artform #computerscience #programming
videos.text: One of the ways that computer science people like to study programming language design is they create something that is often called a "toy" language and this is something to mess around with that won't mess up the real world. Now I'm a contemporary artist and so I decided to create a toy art form, which I call whistlegrafing, in order to better understand all the different art forms and how they come to be.

videos.desc: Answer to @malekpitney here's an in-depth composition tutorial! #arttutorial #poppy #fountainpen #learn 🌺
videos.text: I'm going to take a break from my lunch salad here to answer this interesting question of how to actually compose a whistle graph. Now the step one is to soak up inspiration. I'm outside my house right now, and as you can see there are these beautiful poppy seed flowers that are growing and I'm kind of inspired by them actually. We can take a closer look here. Look how nice these are. They're really cool. Check it out you guys. And then look there's all these buds that are about to pop. Right? This is going to be a whole little patch of poppy flowers in my backyard. And so let's say I want to write a whistle graph about this. I have my inspo over there in the corner and so now step two is to sketch and listen. So I'm going to start sketching. One, two, one, two, three. Do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do, do. Dun, dun, dun, dun, dun. I kind of like that. Dun, dun, dun, dun. And what I can do is I can turn that into like a score. So step three is to score and try to find a tight relationship between the drawing and actually having the lines. I'm going to sketch this poppy a little bit more actually. Let me see. So it kind of looks like that. And then it has all these flowery lines coming out. But these are kind of like, they're kind of chaotic. So I'd have to figure out a way of turning these into steps. So like one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight. You know, maybe eight times I would loop over them and then I would add something in the center to finish the poppy. So I have to turn that into steps, and so at this point I might make a new page And start turning that into steps, so what if we had dent? dent dent dent dent dent dent dent dent dent dent dent dent and then this would continue on dent dent dent dent dent dent So you have all these little undulations, and maybe I could say that's going to be times eight so now I can say like is there that's two a flower That right is There a flower that right and you start to link together the words and the lines And that's basically how you make a whistle graph and then once you kind of finish that you have to record it So everyone can see it and then you have to repeat and memorize it So you really know it by heart and that's basically how it's done

videos.text: I want to give you a quick lecture on the essence of digitality. So if I start doing something like this, like that Grimes TikTok, you know where she's drawing with the shotgun, it's very difficult to figure out, okay, look, how many, if I ask you, how many am I doing, it's really hard to count, right? I'm really just doing one thing and it's really chaotic. But if I start doing something like this, all of a sudden you can count, one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, whoop, six, seven, eight, and this is the kind of stuff that we can put inside a computer. This is what's computable and this is what's not.

videos.desc: logos vs. whistlegraphs
videos.text: Logos are complicated. They're fascist and idealistic. There's only one way to do them. There's no sound or poetry and they enforce a corporate hierarchy. Whistle graphs on the other hand are memorizable. Everyone in your company can do it in their own style and not only that, but it includes music, visuals, and verbal meaning. It builds solidarity in your company.

segments.text: Logos are complicated. They're fascist and idealistic. There's only one way to do them.
There's no sound or poetry and they enforce a corporate hierarchy.
Whistle graphs on the other hand are memorizable. Everyone in your company can do it in their own
style and not only that, but it includes music, visuals, and verbal meaning. It builds solidarity
in your company.

videos.text: welcome back to another episode of stuff I used to think so I used to think that if you wanted to make art or become an artist then you would basically be making stuff mostly for rich people and the thing about rich people is that rich people tend to be very guilty because they have a lot of power and power means that they have the ability to influence the rest of the world so I thought if you make fancy art for rich people who feel guilty then they'll buy that and then they might change some of their ideas and the power flows down the socioeconomic chain and then you can maybe kind of help fix society that way

videos.desc: 🐸📝 from a cool book i'm reading! #lecture #fountainpen #reading #philosophy
videos.text: I was just reading about first and second order observation. So first order observation is like, say you're just looking at a mountain, right? Nice mountain. Well, the mountain is just the mountain that you're seeing, right? Second order observation is basically, say you're looking at a mountain and you're imagining what that mountain looks like as a picture or what that mountain would look like for others. And that's what you do, second order observation, usually right before you take out your cell phone. People didn't always used to do this, actually. What do you mean? They've only started doing it, I think, in the last couple hundred years, according to some philosophers, which is kind of- No way.

segments.text: I was just reading about first and second order observation.
So first order observation is like, say you're just looking at a mountain, right?
Nice mountain.
Well, the mountain is just the mountain that you're seeing, right?
Second order observation is basically, say you're looking at a mountain and you're imagining
what that mountain looks like as a picture or what that mountain would look like for others.
And that's what you do, second order observation, usually right before you take out your cell
phone.
People didn't always used to do this, actually.

They've only started doing it, I think, in the last couple hundred years, according to
some philosophers, which is kind of- No way.

videos.desc: Meet us in Ashland for the next 5 Saturdays starting this weekend! Get a free sketchbook that we customized and draw stuff in the park! #sketch
videos.text: Open up the door, it's me! Hi, we're Whistlegraph! For Art Beyond May 15th to June 12th, we will be at Lithia Park Saturdays from 11 to 1.30, passing out custom sketchbooks and markers to visitors. Sketchbooks are a fantastic tool for encouraging detailed observation of nature and cultivating one's imagination. We will be available to answer any questions and help you get started drive. Hope to see you there!

videos.text: I want to talk to you about why Crayola markers kind of suck. Take a look at this line quality from this Crayola marker. Do you see right there how going over itself caused that little bit of bleed? It's disgusting. Meanwhile, I can take a stabillo and go over itself as many times as I want, and you have this nice, consistent coverage. Even if I take the Neopico Line 3 from Deleter and decide to do a little something, man, I mean, now this is a fucking fine quality marker.

videos.desc: start by mastering your own hand #auteur #film #lecture #artschool #fundamentals
videos.text: I want to make an appeal to drawing in manual labor. A lot of people out there, they want to be in the creative industry. They want to be art directors or auteurs or film people. They think of themselves as having a really good taste. And what they want to do is they want to write a screenplay or do a project that brings a lot of talented folks together. And all those talented folks come together, and it's their skills that actually sort of make the project happen. But if you really want to be a great director or a great auteur, then I would suggest that one of the first things you do is you actually sit down with an empty page and you start to draw. Because if you can't bring together marks on a page from your own hand in a beautiful and satisfying way that makes others happy and that makes you happy, then how are you going to bring together all these other people and make something that keeps an audience happy and also keep those people happy too?

segments.text: I want to make an appeal to drawing in manual labor.
A lot of people out there, they want to be in the creative industry.
They want to be art directors or auteurs or film people.
They think of themselves as having a really good taste.

that brings a lot of talented folks together.
And all those talented folks come together,
and it's their skills that actually sort of make the project happen.
But if you really want to be a great director or a great auteur,
then I would suggest that one of the first things you do is you actually sit down
with an empty page and you start to draw.
Because if you can't bring together marks on a page from your own hand

then how are you going to bring together all these other people
and make something that keeps an audience happy and also keep those people happy too?

videos.desc: analyzing my own #artsymath videos ✒️➕🎵 #art #math #theory #mediatheory
videos.text: One plus two equals three. So artsy math is by far one of my most popular TikTok videos. And at first I didn't really understand why it was popular, but if you look at the sound, you'll realize it's pretty much all six, seven, eight year old kids who do edit and who love it. Kind of creep me out at first, but then I realized that kids who are that age are actually learning arithmetic in school. And to them, this video is kind of politically radical. It's like going way outside the bounds of what they're allowed to do every day. And so in that sense, it's kind of like a useful form of politics.

videos.desc: scary emotions (with subtitles) on the occasion of this drawing being sold ✒️🥺
videos.text: Sometimes people have a lot of really scary emotions inside and those emotions can express themselves in different ways. A lot of people choose to draw or to smush clay or something like that and shape it into different things when they have those emotions but some people never learned how to do that and so they start to smush things or push things or press things that might have emotions themselves and that's not really good because then what happens is other people will get upset and then they'll have a lot of strong emotions and they won't know how to express those emotions either and so they'll start to express them in different places. Places that art shouldn't usually go and that can be a really big a really big problem but it's also okay that that happens.

I wonder what else I could turn it into.

videos.text: when you draw a line you separate one thing from another thing you also define stuff when you draw a line you basically say this is this this is what this thing is that's why drawing is so fundamental it's fundamental because it's basically just about making decisions and the decisions you make are yours those decisions are decisions that you own and you're responsible for all the decisions you make in a drawing for some people that's a lot of responsibility and they can't handle it and they'll give up and say oh i can't draw that way or i'm not very skilled or anything like that but drawing isn't really about being skilled at representing things it's more about being able to handle the different kinds of decisions that you've actually made and and just deal with them

videos.desc: and platform means "fixed form" #media #learnontiktok #arttutorial #doodle
videos.text: Here's some art advice about form and content. The form of something is kind of like a cross between the medium and the genre. For example, drawings of circles would be considered one form. You can get good at the form of drawings of circles. Now, what would the content of that be? Well, it would just simply be a circle, right? The content would be a circle. For example, if you make a painting of a dog. Woof, woof, woof, woof. The form would be figurative painting or animal painting. And the content would be the dog. So what can you do with this information? Well, as an artist, you can stabilize or fix the form or the content. In other words, you can only make figurative paintings and then improve your skills at figurative painting, but have lots of different content inside of that or vice versa. Interesting enough, platform, what are you using right now?

videos.desc: make lines that look like how you think ✒️☺️ #painting #learnontiktok #abstractart #lecture
videos.text: If you want to do interesting abstract art and do it well, then there's a few things that you should know. First of all, you can't just go crazy, like, because if you just go crazy, then you end up actually doing basically the same thing over and over again. For example, you might do a roving line, or you know, you might be making lots of dots, or we might be doing a lot of BF back and forth, right? So you have to develop your own language, but how do you actually do that with abstract art? Well, one of the things you can do is go a little crazy, like that, and then take care of how crazy you went, and you do that quite a lot, and you'll eventually learn how to use your hand better, and actually figure out sort of what your hand in your eyes is trying to tell you. And it doesn't matter if it looks like anything, what matters is how it feels to you and the viewer.

videos.desc: Reply to @blackwidows.gf it's designed that way! #arttheory #artist #tiktoker #creepy #cute 💙💀
videos.text: When coming up with whistle graphs, I decided I wanted to make an art form that was highly intimate that I could memorize that could last for a very, very long time. And so I came to TikTok noticing that most people on TikTok are about three feet to six feet away from the camera. But really intimate relationships, they're very close, right? So I'm a TikToker who's usually about six inches, I'm usually about three to six inches away from the camera. So I want to do things that make you feel scared, but also bring you joy, just how being intimate with someone can make you scared, but also give you a lot of happiness. There's different kinds of relationships that you can have in the world and some are horrible and some are great. But one of the things about art that's special is you can safely explore hell and heaven at the same time.

videos.desc: Reply to @daddy_likesanime Who here has played it and what new ideas do you have for it!? #nopaint #play #painting #digitalart #games 💡❓
videos.text: Oh wow, thank you so much. I'm glad you like my game. For those of you who don't know, I have a video game called No Paint. And basically it only has two buttons. It has a no button and it has a paint button. And what you do is you watch the computer paint something. And if you don't like it, you say no and it cuts it out. And if you do like it, you say paint and it will leave it on the canvas. And basically you can collaborate with your computer and make a nice little picture. You can click on the link in my profile or go to nopaint.art in your web browser if you want to play. And it's designed to help you creatively and help you come up with new images.

segments.text: Oh wow, thank you so much. I'm glad you like my game.
For those of you who don't know, I have a video game called No Paint.
And basically it only has two buttons. It has a no button and it has a paint button.
And what you do is you watch the computer paint something.
And if you don't like it, you say no and it cuts it out.
And if you do like it, you say paint and it will leave it on the canvas.
And basically you can collaborate with your computer and make a nice little picture.
You can click on the link in my profile or go to nopaint.art in your web browser if you want to play.
And it's designed to help you creatively and help you come up with new images.

videos.desc: thoughts on nighttime 🌚✒️ #nails #fiskars #shoe #paypal
videos.text: I have just trimmed my nails with my fiskars and I am feeling so good right now. I want to talk to you about how important it is to exist in the night time. The night time is cool because everybody is sleeping and you can do basically whatever you want. It's important to have time every day where nobody else is around. So you can do stuff like this. Splash, ink around and stuff like that. And make up little songs like... Here is a shoe, here is a shoe. I want to buy some shoes with PayPal. I lost my debit card so I gotta use PayPal. ♪ Got some shoes online ♪

videos.desc: playing by the stream in maine 🐲 #edutok #lecture #artteacher #dragon #river
videos.text: This is such a beautiful river, and here's my dragon! I can pretend that the dragon is hopping around in the river, but in order to do that I need my dragon and I need my river. The cool thing about drawing is I can just draw a line and say well that's my river, and I can put a dot here and call that my dragon, and then I can bounce my dragon along the river.

segments.text: This is such a beautiful river, and here's my dragon!

but in order to do that I need my dragon and I need my river.

and I can put a dot here and call that my dragon, and then I can bounce my dragon along the river.

videos.text: This is a video of a man who's chopping up some little reeds into flutes. The more he chops the reeds up, the more the flutes are going to disappear. videos.text: You know paint is the very best video game to make a painting with and it's now free in the Apple App Store. And while it's downloading, you can go to our Discord and check out all the crazy interesting paintings that all the people are making. Then you can pop it right on open, jump past the title screen and make your first image. If you don't like what it's painting, you can say no, but if you do, you can say paint. Ooh, no. And then when you want to save your picture, you just tap the middle and then tap there to save.

Send this to your friends who keep asking: 'What's a Whistlegraph?' all the time! 😜 #whistlegraphs #cinema #digitalart #generativeart #gif
videos.text: Have you ever thought about the idea that there might be something like animated gifs or even mp4 movie files with audio and animation and everything, but for your actual brain, for your actual brain, well let me tell you guys about whistle graphs. What do you like to come down the stairs? I don't want to cause I ain't too scared. Whistle graphs are basically like movie files that you can compress inside of your mind and then you can load them back at any age, whether you're age 12 or whether you're age 47. Actually, probably most of the whistle graphs you remember at 12 is watching on TikTok. You're definitely going to remember at 47.

videos.desc: nightmare before xmas movie review 🖋
videos.text: My favorite movie growing up was Nightmare Before Christmas. And the reason why is because I loved all the scary characters and I love that it was a mix between two things that I knew really really well and that was Christmas and Halloween and things were surprising they were like blah right but they were also sad like when Jack is sad and hugs Sally and stuff like that. Oh Jack and Sally. Anyway, it was a really good movie. The music was like this is our town of Halloween.

videos.desc: How to change the meaning of digital paintings! #context #digitalart #nft #theory #learnontiktok
videos.text: Now let's say that you sit down and you want to make a digital painting that is two by two pixels and you fill in the square right here. And you can save this as any kind of format as a PNG or as a JPEG or a GIF file. You can save this arrangement of information in any of these image formats, but you could also store this information in your own mind. And so even your brain can be an image format, but the information doesn't have a whole lot of meaning at this resolution until you start playing with where those pixels show up. For example, if it was on the back of my hand, then the meaning changes.

videos.desc: Reply to @siciliannie  that kind of stuff just leads to cliche in my opinion & efforts to make a 1:1 mapping of audio and visuals yield little meaning
videos.text: This is a pretty good question about a whistlegraph alphabet, which is basically the idea that you would have something that would be like ding dong and something like whoop, and then you would make some kind of drawing that would be like ding dong whoop, ding dong whoop, ding dong ding dong ding dong whoop, but early on I realized that that just wasn't going to fly for an entire art form about visuals and sound, and really the best thing to do is to come up with the whole art form of whistlegraphs is to figure out what line matches what sound and that is the art of it, like this, I used to have lots of energy, but now I'm getting old, so I just sit here doodling a bunny in a bowl.

old, so I just sit here doodling a bunny in a bowl.

videos.desc: Reply to @jjjjrbear this is what i think makes them lovable 🙂 #learnontiktok #drawing #arttheory #digitalart
videos.text: Well, perhaps I can convince you to love whistle graphs if you hate whistle graphs. So one of the most interesting things about whistle graphs as an art form is "Would you like to come down those stairs?" I don't want to because I'm too scared. Is that they're memorizable. So one day when Geoffrey here, the master whistle grapher is super super old and super super about to die with really long hair and I have a little lassie sitting on my lap like my grand kid, I'll be able to pull out a pen and show them all my work and perform for them all my whistle graphs that I've ever written and I don't have to pull out some old computer, I don't have to take them to a museum, all the work will be stored in here and if I forget anything then I can refer to the instructions. So whistle graphs are kind of cool, formally speaking, as an art form just because it's kind of like taking painting off of the canvas.

videos.desc: Reply to @_lyndais  if you want to produce new effects in your art then you must also make new methods and techniques... which is what i love! #learn
videos.text: Yeah, whistle graphs are a made-up art form. Pretty much every art form is made up at some point. And if you use an existing art form with an existing method or technique, then usually you're going to produce an already existing emotional effect in the viewer. So if you want to come up with something, a new kind of feeling that a viewer can have, if you want to make the viewer feel new things, then you have to come up with new methods. I was trapped, and I fell down. I broke my head and died.

videos.desc: Reply to @jaketay69 i choose lame on purpose because it's harder & more challenging to start working from the outside in #learnontiktok #artist #pen
videos.text: You guys will have to excuse the noise because I'm in an airport right now But and I also think this comment is maybe just fishing for a reply But one of the things about making art is that there's always going to be things that are really cool Like this guy here, and there's always going to be things that are Super lame different things that you can do right But in my practice, I basically came to the point where I realized that if I if I go for the lame stuff That nobody that I know is working on then I can through art and energy transform that Into super cool stuff and so whistle graphs are actually lame, you know drawing and singing I don't know any other artists in the world really any contemporary artists who are Drawing and singing at the same time And so yeah by default it's it's kind of a lame thing to do But that's the that's the challenge of the art form is to make it cool again
